svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.text: Hardened multi-profile sandbox · Mac (Apple Silicon) → Colima VM → per-profile Docker stacks
• claude login — OAuth device flow (token lands in container)
• gh / glab → use token paste; OAuth callback can't reach sandbox-internal

• Attach to running claude-agent-

, or use devcontainer.json
• Port forwarding (Streamlit/Dash/Jupyter) via docker exec channel — no published ports
Colima VM state (disk image, config, lima dirs)
Your git clones for this profile
no cross-profile credential or session leakage.
rootless Linux VM on the external drive — all Docker lives here
One stack per profile. Concurrent. Shared image, isolated networks & state.

g.text: gh / glab GitHub / GitLab CLIs + git credential helpers

svg.text: Mount points (pre-created in Dockerfile, chown agent:agent)
Bypass virtiofs for perf + correctness (fsync, uid-sensitive writes).

g.text:
macolima-

_vscode-server — VS Code server extracts cleanly

svg.text: the only path out — enforces the shared domain allowlist

g.text: • Caps: SETUID + SETGID only (runs as proxy user)
• HTTP & HTTPS CONNECT on :3128; read_only: true rootfs
• Hot reload of allowed_domains.txt via docker compose restart

svg.text: Each profile is its own Compose project → its own
Run concurrently; ≈8 GB mem per profile. Three profiles ≈ 24 GB.
No IP-level cross-talk. docker exec into the wrong one → "no such container".
Internet — only what's on the allowlist
